name: Oral nicotinamide lowered new nonmelanoma skin cancer rates in high-risk adults
summary: A phase 3 human trial provides the strongest narrow clinical signal in the current DNA repair enhancement baseline.
statement: In high-risk immunocompetent adults, 500 mg nicotinamide twice daily lowered the rate of new nonmelanoma skin cancers during 12 months of treatment.
population_or_model: Adults with at least two nonmelanoma skin cancers in the previous 5 years
quantitative_note: The new nonmelanoma skin cancer rate was 23% lower than placebo at 12 months (95% CI 4 to 38; P=0.02); actinic keratoses were also lower during treatment.
caveats: The population was selected for high skin-cancer risk and was not immunosuppressed.
The endpoint is skin cancer prevention, not broad aging or systemic DNA repair capacity.
The trial found no evidence of benefit after nicotinamide was discontinued.
